_comment: COMMITTED ANSWER KEY for the worker-anchor comprehension eval (fn-83.3, R9). Binary anchor-comprehension questions per frozen task; grading is against THIS key (bundle-vs-statusquo agreement is NOT the metric — both-wrong must fail). `accept` = case-insensitive regexes that must ALL match the subject's answer line for a point. `kind: non_satisfies` marks the question answerable ONLY from a spec section OUTSIDE the task's satisfies/coverage list (guards any future bundle-filtering regression). Key is FROZEN once generated; new tasks/questions append.

questions.question: Per the parent spec's Requirement coverage table, which R-IDs does task fn-64.3 cover? List the R-IDs.
The task's acceptance requires updating which three Linear reference docs with exact mutation/field names?
Per the parent spec's Boundaries, is transitive/graph expansion of dependency edges in scope? Answer yes or no, and state what IS projected.
questions.answer: No — only direct depends_on_epics edges are projected

questions.question: What is the status of this task's dependency task fn-64.2?
Which GraphQL mutation implements the relation write on the GraphQL rung, and with which relation type value?
Per the parent spec's R6 (NOT one of fn-64.3's covered R-IDs), what must tracker-sync do with existing tracker relations it cannot prove it created?
questions.answer: Preserve them — never remove relations it cannot prove it created

questions.question: When listing relations for dedup, which TWO GraphQL connections must be queried, and which explicit argument does each require?
questions.answer: relations AND inverseRelations, each with an explicit first: argument

questions.question: Which R-IDs does the task's satisfies list name? List them all.
Which file does the task's Files line name as the implementation target?
Per the parent spec's Boundaries, may the trivial-diff triage judge gain cursor as a backend? Answer yes or no, and name the backends it keeps.
questions.answer: No — the triage judge stays codex|copilot

questions.question: What is the status of this task's dependency task fn-74.1?
Under what exact condition may a cursor re-review resume via --resume, and what happens on a cross-backend receipt?
questions.answer: Resume only when the prior receipt's mode == "cursor"; a cross-backend receipt starts a fresh session

questions.question: Per the parent spec's R2 (NOT in this task's satisfies list), what must BackendSpec.parse("cursor:gpt-5.5-high:high") do?
questions.answer: Raise — effort is rejected for cursor

questions.question: Which receipt key must cursor receipts NEVER contain?
Which R-IDs does the task's satisfies list name? List them all.
Which TWO skills' workflow-rp.md files appear in the task's Files list?
Is Codex-mirror regeneration in scope for this task? Answer yes or no, and name which task owns it.
questions.answer: No — canonical files only; fn-81.4 owns the mirror regen

questions.question: What is the status of this task's dependency task fn-81.1?
What is the default value of the fix-loop iteration cap, and which environment variable overrides it?
Per the parent spec's R12 (NOT in this task's satisfies list), which skill's scout-model prose must be made to match the agent frontmatter ground truth?
What staging discipline replaces `git add -A` in both RP fix loops?
questions.answer: Snapshot-scoped staging — git status --porcelain before/after, stage only paths that changed between snapshots
